scope: HTTP delivery, static asset versioning, frontend startup request reduction, lazy greeting module loading, smart prefetch, virtual-list instrumentation, and developer diagnostics only.

details: Added centralized HTTP optimization middleware.
Added Brotli compression when accepted by the browser.
Added gzip fallback when Brotli is unavailable.
Compression is limited to text, JSON, JavaScript, CSS, SVG, XML, and HTML responses.
Non-compressible responses stream through without being buffered.
Added Keep-Alive headers.
Added ETag and Last-Modified handling for versioned HTML entry points.
Authenticated API responses are marked no-store/no-cache/private.
Added automatic build hash generation from public asset mtimes and sizes.
HTML entry points receive script/link URLs with ?v=<buildHash>.
Static non-HTML assets use long-lived immutable browser cache headers.
HTML documents remain no-store to preserve authenticated SPA/session correctness.
Parallelized org settings, connection check, and /api/portal/me startup work.
Removed duplicate /api/portal/me startup request by passing the already-loaded user context into loadProfile().
Preserved permission-gated sidebar initialization.
Removed eager greeting.css and greeting.js from CRM and Admin initial HTML.
Greeting assets are loaded only when the greeting mount is rendered.
Reports, Dashboards, and Admin remain separate route/page bundles as before.
Existing object-list virtualization was preserved.
Render timing instrumentation was added around table render and virtual window render.
No UI behavior, sorting, filtering, selection, edit/delete, or infinite-scroll semantics were changed.
Likely next object lists are prefetched after the current object successfully renders.
Prefetch runs through requestIdleCallback or timeout fallback.
Prefetch cancels when user interaction occurs before the idle callback runs.
Prefetch respects object read permissions and existing auth token handling.
Prefetched list payloads are reused by the existing CRM list cache path.
Added window.getPerformanceStats() in localhost/dev mode.

Stats include client cache hits/misses, deduped requests, stale responses, API timings, render timings, resource transfer/encoded/decoded bytes, compression ratio, module initialization, and JS heap data where available.
Existing admin-only /api/debug/performance-audit remains the backend source for Salesforce/Supabase timing details.

before.virtualization: present before this sprint in the SPA state branch

after.largeListBehavior: visible window plus buffers instead of full table DOM

commands.command: inline script parse check for public/admin.html

commands.notes: Git emitted line-ending normalization warnings only; no whitespace errors were reported.

liveBrowserTiming.reason: This measurement artifact records implementation-level before/after and static validation. Runtime timings can now be captured with window.getPerformanceStats() and the existing admin-only /api/debug/performance-audit endpoint.

remainingBottlenecks: Live browser measurements should be captured across cold and warm loads after deployment because compression ratios depend on browser cache state and payload shape.
Salesforce/Supabase server-side timings remain available through the existing performance audit endpoint and should be compared with client resource timings during final QA.
Very large JSON API payloads are still constrained by payload size and parsing cost even when compressed; this sprint did not change API schemas or backend pagination behavior.
